series_ref: SERIES-FLOW-MODEL (with Living Claim Rail) — surfaced at V3 review_ready, per directive
one_line_intent: DECIDE (the operating-model evolution): two human gates per SERIES instead of N cards per volume. GATE A Series-Lock (front): KM ratifies locked outlines + standard-rail + seeded claim registry; FREEZE precondition (no draft until ratified). FLOW: volumes run the FULL rail (3 editorial+UX+Tech/Arch 6-gate+seeit+review_ready_contract) autonomously, ZERO intermediate KM cards. GATE B Batch-Review (end): one card w/ per-vol Review Briefs + the SUBSTANTIVE-QUALITY signal (Cold-Reader/adversarial verdict as a structural metric per claim-family = the #4 control enforcing 'quality must not drop'). Integrity backbone = Living Claim Rail v0.1 (declare/prove/watch/revise; build-first on V3). Quality SAME-OR-TIGHTER (boards+Gate6+contract unchanged per vol; only human card-count drops). Distribution lane held visible/parallel. No new boards/packets (bloat-guard). Rollout: claim-rail v0.1 on V3 -> S3 two-gate -> S5-S9 batch. Full: artifacts/GB_Series_Flow_Model_2026-06-18.md.
